seo.metaDescription: Pelagic Earth's mission: transforming ocean pollution into sustainable solutions through research and innovation.

sections.heroCopy: What we discard holds the power to endure.

sections.taglineText: Waste is in the eye of the beholder.
sections.body: Pelagic exists to reframe waste as a resource. What others discard, we turn into premium construction products that shape sustainable communities and build closed loop partnerships.

sections.title: Our unique solution
sections.description: Pelagic.Earth deploys Manufacturing as a Service (MaaS) designed from the systems level down to transform discarded plastic into product.

items.title: Infrastructure
items.description: Pelagic create decentralised infrastructure - production hubs colocated with soft plastic processing and modular deployable factories. Portable Pelagic mod.factories, each in a 40-foot container, can be air-lifted, trucked, or transported directly to plastic waste, whether a remote beach or a building site.

items.title: Scaled
items.description: This forms a decentralised and independent network of production, connected and dynamic; to elastically meet demand and supply with real-time carbon reporting and impact measurement

items.title: Product
items.description: The output—a contemporary, functional product for sale or immediate use, even on the site it is produced.

sections.title: Pelagic.Earth introduces Pave
sections.description: The first in Australia. Higher compression strength and flexural strength, designed to the highest quality and standard. 100% recycled content. Each metre of product removes over 30kgs of plastic from the environment.

sections.title: Act locally, impact globally
sections.copy: From our first installations in 2025 to more than 20 mod.factories worldwide by 2029, Pelagic is on track to upcycle 250,000 tons of plastic waste into building products scaling local impact into a global circular system.
sections.statistic01Copy: Each time we deploy a single m2 of Pelagic pavers, we sequester over 30kg of landfill/ocean plastic from the environment.
sections.statistic02Copy: Each Mod.factory processes 500 tonnes per year of soft plastic

sections.description: In 2025 we exhibited at Milan Design Week. Alongside our physical exhibition we launched a digital companion—conceived as both exhibit and experiment, the platform invites visitors to reframe discarded plastics as a potential vast commodity; a story of hope and growth.

items.question: What makes Pelagic pavers different from traditional materials?
items.answer:
Pelagic pavers bricks represent a transformative approach to building materials, turning ocean plastic waste into high-performance structural pavers.

Key differentiators:

• Circular material: Each square meter of removes 30kg of plastic from the environment, creating value from waste.
• Performance standards: Engineered to meet or exceed traditional building material standards for durability and strength.
• Local production: Our portable mod.factories enable on-site manufacturing, reducing transportation and carbon footprint.
• Certified quality: Rigorous testing ensures compliance with building codes and industry certifications.

Unlike traditional bricks or concrete, our pavers create a closed-loop system where waste becomes a resource.
items.contactText: To discuss a project or pilot, reach out via

items.question: How can architects, developers, or councils partner with Pelagic?
items.answer:
Pelagic partners with architects, developers, and councils to turn local plastic waste into certified building materials.

We offer collaboration opportunities across three areas:

• Design integration: specify Pelagic pavers or systems in sustainable building projects.
• Circular manufacturing: establish on-site or regional micro-factories to process local waste streams.
• Policy and education: work with councils to pilot closed-loop infrastructure and community recycling programs.
items.contactText: To discuss a project or pilot, reach out via

items.question: When will Pelagic pavers and mod.factories be available for preorder or purchase?
items.answer:
Pelagic is actively scaling production with installations planned globally.

Availability:

• Preorders now open: Secure your place in our production queue for 2025-2026 deployments.
• Early access programs: Partner with us for pilot projects and receive priority access.
• Custom deployments: Work with our team to deploy a mod.factory tailored to your region's waste stream.
• Commercial scale: Enterprises and municipalities can arrange bulk orders for infrastructure projects.

Connect with us to discuss timelines, pricing, and capacity planning for your specific project needs.
items.contactText: To discuss a project or pilot, reach out via

items.question: Can Pelagic pavers be certified and do they meet building codes?
items.answer:
products undergoe comprehensive testing to meet international building standards and certifications.

Certifications and compliance:

• Structural performance: Engineered to meet or exceed ASTM standards for load-bearing applications.
• Durability testing: Accelerated aging tests confirm long-term performance and weather resistance.
• Material safety: Non-toxic composition tested through third-party laboratory testing.
• Building codes: Compliant with national and international construction standards.

Our quality assurance team works continuously with certification bodies to ensure materials meet or exceed all applicable building codes.
items.contactText: To discuss a project or pilot, reach out via

items.question: What is the environmental impact of producing Pelagic pavers?
items.answer:
Pelagic pavers create a net positive environmental impact by transforming ocean plastic waste into durable building materials.

Environmental benefits:

• Waste diversion: Each square meter removes 30kg of plastic from marine environments.
• Carbon reduction: Our production process uses less energy than traditional brick manufacturing.
• Water conservation: Minimal water usage compared to conventional concrete production.
• Circular economy: Creates value from waste, reducing reliance on virgin materials.
• Local production: Portable mod.factories minimize transportation emissions.

Life cycle assessment shows Pelagic pavers have lower embodied carbon and environmental impact compared to traditional building materials.
items.contactText: To discuss a project or pilot, reach out via

items.question: How do the costs of Pelagic pavers compare to traditional building materials?
items.answer:
Pelagic offers competitive pricing with additional value through sustainability and performance.

Pricing considerations:

• Material costs: Comparable to premium traditional materials, with long-term value benefits.
• Installation: Similar installation processes reduce labor costs compared to specialized systems.
• Durability: Longer lifespan reduces replacement and maintenance costs over time.
• Sustainability credits: Potential LEED credits and environmental certifications may offset costs.
• Brand value: Demonstrate commitment to circular economy and environmental responsibility.

Contact our sales team for detailed pricing, volume discounts, and project-specific quotes.

journalArticles.fullIntroduction: A short article intro for layout verification. We are using this seeded entry to test every journal detail block across desktop and mobile breakpoints.
journalArticles.listingIntroduction: Reference article used to test journal detail layout against design.

contentBlocks.copy: This style can be used for a breakout block of text that might be a call out, quote or something similar. Contemporary building products and alternative manufacturing using ocean and landfill bound soft plastics through.

**You can insert a title if you want, but it’s optional for your nose to your lover’s collar and been completely overcome by their smell? Your pulse quickens. You can’t get your words out**

This is a repeatable component of full width text. Use as many times as you like throughout a journal page. Let's give waste new meaning, connect communities, stabilise markets, and reorient our waste management and industrial systems. And let's do it at scale. Anchored in the idea that 'waste is in the eye of the beholder' — waste is a cultural proposition, an industrial byproduct, an ecosystem out of balance, a misalignment of design x [time + function]. 

An installation using ocean plastics and other end-of-life plastic as an alternative to concrete. An immersive space to reflect on design, materiality, waste an external link explore what fundamentally matters in good design and how to apply it at scale within a rapidly changing world.
This block is used to test a left-copy and right-image pairing. It should align vertically in desktop view and stack naturally on mobile.
A mirrored pair with the image on the left and the text on the right. Use this to verify alternating rhythm and section pacing through the article body.

The next section provides additional paragraph flow to validate typography scaling and spacing across breakpoints. This ensures that multi-paragraph copy remains legible and balanced between media-heavy modules.

journalArticles.title: Pelagic.Earth x Parley
journalArticles.fullIntroduction: We partnered with Parley to take bold steps toward building a better future for the ocean. This collaboration explores how material innovation can support coastal communities and circular construction.
journalArticles.listingIntroduction: We partnered with Parley to take bold steps towards building a better future for the ocean.

contentBlocks.copy: Waste is not an endpoint. It is a design material waiting for a new system.
Our model links local waste streams to local manufacturing capacity so material can stay in circulation where it is needed most.

contentBlocks.copy01: We design for deployment, from dense urban projects to remote regions.
contentBlocks.copy02: Each deployment turns plastic waste into durable product with traceable impact.

contentBlocks.copy: A modular production system allows us to bring manufacturing closer to the waste source.
Partnerships across design, logistics, and policy are what make circular systems practical at scale.

globalSetting.footerTagline: Waste is in the eye of the beholder.

seo.metaDescription: Pelagic Earth's mission: turning ocean-bound plastic into design-forward construction materials through circular systems.

sections.title: Mission
sections.copy: Pelagic is built on a core belief that waste isn't just a liability, it's a resource waiting to be unlocked.
That's the number of tons of plastic waste the UNEP estimates escapes into the environment each year.

sections.leadCopy: That's enough to create a 1 meter wide plastic belt wrapped around the entire planet, fifty eight times.

sections.copy:
This is systemic.

Communities are overwhelmed, ecosystems are polluted.

Together we pay a collective price for a linear model that treats 'away' like a real place.

But waste doesn't have to be the end of the story. Perhaps instead, a beginning.

Each discarded item has the potential to be something more valuable, more hopeful, than before.
We've built a process and technology, driven by this vision, transforming waste into a valuable commodity.

By 2029, Pelagic aims to be converting 100,000 tons of discarded soft plastics into premium structural products that create profit, employment and relief for our environment.

That's our why.

sections.copy01: The Pelagic team is a group of passionate, creative people united by a simple belief - waste does not have to be the end of the story. We can make a difference.

sections.quote: I've always believed a systems view of culture, society, economics and ecology can help discover novel ideas to bring change. Pelagic is built on that notion.
sections.name: Philippa Abbott, Founder

seo.metaDescription: Pelagic Mod.factory is a mobile micro-factory that converts ocean-bound plastic into build-ready material systems at source.

sections.headline: Mod.factory is production capability within a 40ft shipping container.
sections.subheadline: Designed to drop into any location from a remote beach to a construction site. Mod.factory makes pavers at the plastic waste source.

sections.title: Bringing production to the source of the plastic waste.
sections.intro: Resulting from an extensive systems design process, Mod.factory offers flexibility, localised efficiencies and modularity for distributed manufacturing.

cards.title: Waste diversion
cards.description: Create solutions for increasing soft plastic waste and a lack of appropriate infrastructure.

cards.title: Construction
cards.description: Convert plastic waste into high demand construction products on location, condensing supply chains and timelines.

cards.title: Community
cards.description: Create local business partnerships, generating livelihoods with local training and engagement.

cards.title: Municipal Waste Hubs
cards.description: Process plastics at source to reduce landfill and logistics overheads.

sections.title: Deploying Mod.factory at your location
sections.intro: A structured implementation pathway from feasibility through installation and first-day operation.

steps.title: Feasibility
steps.description: We run a desktop review of your site, logistics and feedstock to confirm fit and produce a clear go/no-go recommendation.

steps.title: Site preparation
steps.description: We prepare required documentation and support ground works so access and safety measures are ready before delivery.

steps.title: Installation and turn on
steps.description: We manufacture, deliver, install and commission your Mod.factory so the plant can begin processing waste quickly.

bottomCopyBlocks.copy: Deployment timelines vary by permitting and site readiness, with most projects moving from scoping to operation in a staged rollout.
Pelagic teams work with councils, developers and operators to align local feedstock, demand and manufacturing requirements.

sections.label: Technical brief
sections.title: Let's get technical.
sections.intro: Factory specifications and site requirements for deployment planning.

specs.label: Electrical
specs.value: 3-phase 400V supply. 325A minimum load, up to 625A at full processing capacity.

specs.label: Site requirements

sections.title: Follow us as we scale globally
sections.intro: Our interactive microsite tracks mod.factory installations and explores the impact we can have together.

sections.statement: By 2028 we aim to have 30 Mod.factories sequestering a total of 15,000 tons of plastic out of our environment every single day.
